Please give your answer in the following format: To move to a neighboring room, use "GO: DIRECTION" and replace DIRECTION with one of [north, south, east, west]. To stop the exploration, answer with "DONE" instead. Omit any other text.
Here is an example:
You are in the Kitchen. Currently available directions: south, west. What is your next instruction?
GO: west
You have made a step and entered a Lobby. Currently available directions: east, north. What is your next instruction?
GO: north
...
You have made a step and entered a Bedroom. Currently available directions: south. What is your next instruction?
DONE

GO: west

You have made a step and entered Bedroom. Currently available directions: south. What is your next instruction?

GO: south
You have made a step and entered Garage. Currently available directions: north, west. What is your next instruction?
GO: west
You have made a step and entered Attic. Currently available directions: north, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: north
You have made a step and entered Sunroom. Currently available directions: north, south, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: north
You have made a step and entered Game room. Currently available directions: south. What is your next instruction?
GO: south
You have made a step and entered Sunroom. Currently available directions: north, south, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: east
You have made a step and entered Recreation room. Currently available directions: north, south, west, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: east
You have made a step and entered Pantry. Currently available directions: west, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: east
You have made a step and entered Dining room. Currently available directions: west. What is your next instruction?
